experiment: F-EXP10 label-drift fix: temporal label snapshots for trajectory analysis
prior_work: L-946 (S420): MIXED collapse 23%→4.1% is 40% measurement artifact from label drift. L-927 (S417): three UCB1 failure modes (rich-get-richer, log compression, score≠evenness). L-776 (S391): structural weights R²=-0.089, UCB1 exploit 12x better.
method: Cross-era label analysis: compute outcome labels using only data available at each measurement period (label_at_time) vs all-time recomputation (label_current). Compare MIXED share, domain rankings, and scoring deltas across S300-S384 (Era 1) vs S385-S424 (Era 2). N=420 lanes across 33 domains.
expect: Label drift affects >20% of dispatch history. label_at_time vs label_current shows different domain ranking. MIXED share stable (26%) with temporal labels vs 4.1% current.

degraded_apparent.pattern: PROVEN→MIXED due to low N in Era 2, not performance decline
degraded_apparent.note: 3/4 have 100% or 75% merge rate in Era 2 — label is sample-size artifact

scoring_impact.max_rank_shift: ±3 positions for domains at label boundary
scoring_impact.note: MIXED→PROVEN drift reduces score by 1.5; PROVEN→MIXED drift increases by 1.5

mixed_share_trajectory.key_driver: META (37 lanes) was MIXED at S363, promoted to PROVEN by S420
mixed_share_trajectory.note: Temporal labels stabilize MIXED share, confirming L-946

findings.drift_rate_confirmed: 27.3% of domains (9/33) show label drift between eras — CONFIRMS >20% expectation. Label drift is endemic to any threshold-based classification system applied to accumulating data.
findings.measurement_vs_dispatch_distinction: Critical insight: label drift is a MEASUREMENT artifact for retrospective trajectory analysis, NOT a dispatch bug. UCB1 using all-time data for live scoring is correct (more data = better estimate). The bug is in how F-EXP10's own trajectory measurements evaluate dispatch performance. Retrospective analysis must use label_at_time, not label_current.
findings.mixed_share_stable_confirmed: MIXED share stabilizes at ~26% with temporal labels vs 4.1% with current labels. L-946's 40% artifact estimate is CONSERVATIVE — actual artifact is larger for MIXED specifically because META (the largest domain, 37 lanes) crossed the PROVEN threshold mid-measurement.
findings.sample_size_confound: 4/9 drifted domains show PROVEN→MIXED regression SOLELY from low N in Era 2 (N<5). Their actual merge rates are equal or higher. The OUTCOME_MIN_N=3 threshold is too low to prevent this — at N=3-4, a single ABANDONED lane flips the label. Consider OUTCOME_MIN_N=5 for label stability.
findings.scoring_sensitivity: Each label drift creates ±1.5 score delta (MIXED bonus 2.0 vs PROVEN bonus 0.5). For domains near the UCB1 median, this can shift rank by 3+ positions. The MIXED_BONUS=2.0 amplifies label instability because the bonus is 4x the PROVEN bonus.
findings.two_fix_targets: Fix 1 (measurement): trajectory analysis tools should snapshot labels at measurement time. Fix 2 (dispatch stability): raise OUTCOME_MIN_N from 3 to 5 to reduce small-N label flips.

different_ranking.actual: ±3 rank positions for boundary domains, ±1.5 score delta

actual: All 3 expectations confirmed. Label drift affects 27.3% of domains (9/33, N=420 lanes). MIXED share stabilizes at 26% with temporal labels vs 4.1% with current labels. Critical distinction: drift is a measurement artifact for trajectory analysis, not a dispatch bug. UCB1 live scoring correctly uses all-time data. Fix targets: (1) snapshot labels in trajectory tools, (2) raise OUTCOME_MIN_N from 3→5.
diff: All predictions confirmed but the finding is more nuanced: the drift is split between measurement-artifact (trajectory analysis) and dispatch-scoring (live ranking). Both are affected but for different reasons. Sample-size confound (N<5) is the dominant driver for 4/9 drifted domains — raising OUTCOME_MIN_N would eliminate 44% of observed drift.
